required_complexity_patterns.notes: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click
Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form
Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell
Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only
Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers

reference_templates.title: Radio styled as buttons
reference_templates.notes: Native radios hidden; visible pill buttons styled via CSS class

reference_templates.title: List item fields

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell Required: Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only Required: Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers Reference DOM (Radio styled as buttons): Native radios hidden; visible pill buttons styled via CSS class Reference DOM (Split date selects): Start date as three separate day/month/year select elements Reference DOM (List item fields): Each field wrapped in li inside ul list structure Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: details-accordion, decoy-form, iframe-apply, table-layout, split-phone. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: unstyled div soup with inline styles only. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Radio styled as buttons; Split date selects; List item fields.
